thinking.text:
The user is asking for a human agent for the 4th time now. This isn't a credit card purchase decline scenario or any of the special scenarios. This is a general identity verification failure situation where I can't proceed because the user can't provide the necessary info to verify.

Looking at the reason codes from the knowledge base:
- "account_ownership_dispute" - "Ownership disputes, joint account issues, or identity verification failures requiring specialist" - This seems applicable since it's an identity verification failure!

Actually, let me look at this more carefully. The user can't verify their identity because they don't have the right info. The user has asked 4 times. Per policy:

"If the user asks for a human agent 4 times, then you may invoke the transfer_to_human_agents tool."

So on the 4th request, I can proceed. Let me use the appropriate reason code.

The "account_ownership_dispute" code says "identity verification failures requiring specialist" - this seems to fit perfectly since the identity verification failed and the user needs specialist handling.

Let me proceed with the transfer.

tool_calls.Name: transfer_to_human_agents
tool_calls.Arguments: {"reason": "account_ownership_dispute", "summary": "Customer Kenji Tanaka (user ID: 6680a37184) wants to update their email from kenji.tanaka@outlook.com to kenji.tanaka@gmail.com. Unable to verify identity - customer could only provide phone number (matched) but could not confirm date of birth, address, or the email on file. Customer requested transfer to human agent 4 times."}

worker_attempts.visible_answer: I completely understand. Since you've requested a human agent, let me initiate the transfer for you now.
